The experiment apparatus utilizes reservoir shapes and a high voltage electric field to help drive the gas phase to a desired location and prevent bubbles from blocking outlet/inlet channels. A mixing filter in one reservoir and a high-voltage electrode in the other could potentially help researchers understand the occurrence of

title: Satellite for Natural and Artificial Plumes (SNAP)
description: The Satellite for Natural and Artificial Plumes (SNAP) project aims to detect and classify natural and anthropogenic plumes in real time, which could enable timely tracking of short-timescale events (current solutions rely on post-processing of downlinked data). Specific plume cases can be generalized to any event detectable by computer-vision techniques. SNAP will use gimbaled cameras to identify and track plumes. The data from the cameras (visible to mid-infrared [IR]) will be processed by
center: Texas A&M Systems Engineering, Architecture and Knowledge Lab
partners: Texas A&M Systems Engineering, Architecture and Knowledge Lab

title: Hydrogen Electrical Power System
description:
Teledyne’s hydrogen electrical power system is a fuel cell technology designed to be a cost-effective energy storage system that can integrate with a lunar habitat. Tests aim to qualify the technology in a space environment and validate its functionality; data from flights could contribute to the system’s ability to survive a lunar night. This has the potential to benefit NASA missions, the commercial space industry, and other government agencies.

Summary of September 18, 2025 Fli

title: Electrodynamic Regolith Conveyor
description: The Electrodynamic Regolith Conveyor is designed to move lunar regolith particles with the use of dynamic electric fields rather than conventional rotating or vibrating actuation. The technology concept involves generating electric fields by placing alternating high voltage on electrodes of the conveyor surface. A suborbital flight test aims to enable researchers to assess and advance this dust-tolerant technology for future in situ resource utilization (ISRU) operations on the Moon.

title: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. Proposal to the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Space Technology Announcement of Collaborative Opportunity (ACO)
description: Advanced re-entry and recovery technologies are critical for reliably transporting humans and payloads to and from space. Testing aims to mature these capabilities on a Rocket Lab vehicle. Its recoverable first stage may ultimately provide access to high-speed, high-temperature re-entry environments for testing. This has the potential to benefit NASA missions and the commercial space industry

title: Electrophysiology Recording of Neuronal Networks During Suborbital Spaceflight
description: The Electrophysiology Recording of Neuronal Networks During Suborbital Spaceflight demonstration will assess the ability of an electrophysiological measurement system to monitor live neuronal networks in space. During a Blue Origin suborbital rocket flight, a commercial electrophysiology measurement platform will monitor a culture of neurons and collect data for post flight analysis. The objectives of the experiment are to validate the measurement platform in space and also to analyze the ele
center: IMEC USA Nanoelectronics Design Center Inc
partners: IMEC USA Nanoelectronics Design Center Inc
The University of Texas at El Paso

title: Ground-Based Telemetry Antenna Arraying
description:
Our project aims to identify what ground-based high-data-rate telemetry requires using antenna system arraying techniques. While some COTS/GOTS solutions exist, they are more specifically designed for satellite orbital tracking and data reception. Our focus is unique- we aim to determine what additional hardware and software is required while operating under the high flight dynamics of a sounding rocket, a challenge that has not yet been fully explored.

The Antenna System Array will co
technologyArea: Communications, Navigation, and Orbital Debris Tracking and Characterization Systems

title: Passive fabrication of FUV bandpass filters for HWO spectrograph instrumentation
description: This effort supports the production of reflective far-ultraviolet (FUV) band-selecting filters fo FUV multi-object spectrograph applications. We will utilize a novel reactive physical vapor deposition (rPVD) method, which has been previously proven to produce high-efficient reflective FUV broadband films, to fabricate these filters. This effort will target the development of a high-efficiency FUV bandpass filter operating at and extended spectral range of 160-200 nm.

title: Intelligent Extensible Mission Architectures (IEMA) Verification and Validation
description: This project aims to develop a research-driven framework for measuring and mitigating risk and establishing infusion standards for integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) actors into aerospace systems by examining usage through the Intelligent Extensible Mission Architecture(IEMA) sample AI mission. We will investigate the current state of Verification and Validation (V&V) of AI within the aerospace domain and, using our findings, design a testing framework and apply it to the IEMA example.

title: Planetary Trajectory Design Using Generative AI Tools
description: Developing planetary trajectories involves massive trade studies over large spans of launch dates, propulsion systems, gravity assist bodies, launch vehicles, etc. Such work requires rapid computation and optimization of many thousands of trajectories to characterize the solution space. This work requires regular development of in-house software (primarily the Evolutionary Mission Trajectory Generator, EMTG) to solve new types of problems often introduced by each new project. Working in this

title: Investigation of Noncondensable and Autogenous Unsettled Cryogenic Pressurization Schemes in Reduced Gravity
description: The Investigation of Non-condensable and Autogenous Unsettled Cryogenic Pressurization Schemes in Reduced Gravity will demonstrate the pressure control capability of a cryogenic liquid storage tank in reduced gravity. It will examine pressurization directly to the ullage (the top of the liquid’s surface) as well a ssubsurface pressurization. Over three parabolic flights, researchers will investigate the performance of both a non-condensable and autogenous gas in these two schemes. This data i

title: Physical vapor deposition coating uniformity optimization for HWO viability assessment
description: This effort aims to optimize the coating uniformity of conventional line-of-sight coating deposition methods for HWO applications. We will explore the use of a custom fixture to explore the application of conventional aluminum (Al) + magnesium fluoride (MgF2) coatings in a dynamic planetary coating configuration. This configuration will allow for rotation and revolution of a mounted optic relative to the center of the chamber while the coating deposition sources remain static during the coati

title: JOEEE Hopping Cartwheeling Mobility Platform
description: JOEEE (Jumping Orientable Extreme Environment Explorer) is a novel planetary surface mobility platform that uses internal brake-able flywheels to enable cartwheeling, hopping, and precision pointing. JOEEE’s unique maneuvering capability enables deployment of scientific and engineering payloads in terrain and environments inaccessible to traditional wheeled rovers or rocket hoppers. The goals of this proposal are to complete the demonstration of the mobility system in hardware prototypes (TRL

title: AI/ML-Driven HyperMapping with Hyperspectral Precise Pointing Optical Sensor for Decadal Survey Mission Pathfinder Activities
description: This effort will develop and test advanced Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) techniques for path planning/adaptive observing using a suite of airborne terrestrial and ocean remote sensing (RS) instruments. An airborne computational and instrument capability would be used for both reactive and limited memory AI tasks. The present focus is to transform the observing capability of a gimbled dual-axis hyper spectrometer (180-1200nm, 4096 wavebands) from that of a deterministic prog

title: Capturing Human Adaptations in Novel Gravitational Environments in Space (CHANGES)
description: With the Capturing Human Adaptations in Novel Gravitational Environments in Space (CHANGES) demonstration, researchers will test a suite of sensors and a display technology designed as a non-pharmaceutical countermeasure for space adaptation sickness (SAS). Tasks displayed on a knee-mounted monitor will drive the head and gaze through a series of motions to invoke neuro-vestibular discomfort. Using an elastic headset, the technology provides a visual alert when head motion exceeds preset limi

title: Three-Dimensional Plume-Surface Interaction and Crater Formation Dynamic Measurements in Reduced Gravity Environments
description: The Three-Dimensional Plume-Surface Interaction and Crater Formation Dynamic Measurements experiment will study plume-surface interaction (PSI) in reduced and lunar gravity conditions. Future landings on the Moon pose significant risk because the high-velocity retrorocket exhaust plume disturbs the surface regolith during approach and landing, resulting in cratering, severe lunar dust blowing, and particle ejecting. These flight tests aim to gather data under realistic conditions to better un
technologyArea: Integrated Modeling and Simulation for EDL

title: Thermal Transport Characterization of Phase-Change Material (PCM) Infused in Annular Passage Filled with Additively Manufactured Metal Foam Obtained Through Octet-Shaped Unit Cell Reticulation Under Micro-gravity Conditions
description: The Thermal Transport Characterization of PCM Infused in Annular Passage Filled with Additively Manufactured Metal Foam (AAMF) project aims to design, model, and test a novel phase change material (PCM) that combines high heat absorption of phase change material with high thermal conductivity of Octet-shaped metal foams. The research aims to enhance the fundamental understanding of phase change processes under micro-gravity conditions, and to address the PCM incorporation challenges through PCM
